Generate forecasts from a fitted milt model

Source: R/model-interface.R
milt_forecast.Rd

Generate forecasts from a fitted milt model

Usage

milt_forecast(
  model,
  horizon,
  level = c(80, 95),
  num_samples = NULL,
  future_covariates = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

model

A fitted MiltModel.

horizon

Positive integer. Number of steps ahead to forecast.

level

Numeric vector of confidence levels for prediction intervals. Default c(80, 95).

num_samples

Integer or NULL. Number of sample paths to draw for probabilistic forecasts. NULL returns point forecasts only.

future_covariates

A MiltSeries of future-known covariates, or NULL.

...

Additional arguments forwarded to the backend's forecast() method.

Value

A MiltForecast object.

See also

milt_fit(), milt_accuracy()

Other model: milt_backtest(), milt_compare(), milt_cv(), milt_ensemble(), milt_fit(), milt_local_model(), milt_model(), milt_predict(), milt_refit(), milt_residuals()

Examples

# \donttest{
s   <- milt_series(AirPassengers)
fct <- milt_model("naive") |> milt_fit(s) |> milt_forecast(horizon = 12)
#> Fitting <MiltNaive> model…
#> Done in 0s.
# }
